title: Jinja2 Meta-Introspection
content:
This module provides functions for introspecting Jinja2 templates.

### Key Exports

- **`find_undeclared_variables(ast: nodes.Template) -> t.Set[str]`**: Returns a set of all variables in the AST that will be looked up from the context at runtime. This is useful for static analysis and understanding template dependencies.

    ```python
    from jinja2 import Environment, meta
    env = Environment()
    ast = env.parse('{% set foo = 42 %}{{ bar + foo }}')
    undeclared = meta.find_undeclared_variables(ast)
    # undeclared will be {'bar'}
    ```

- **`find_referenced_templates(ast: nodes.Template) -> t.Iterator[t.Optional[str]]`**: Finds all referenced templates from the AST, including hardcoded extensions, inclusions, and imports. It yields `None` for dynamic references.

    ```python
    from jinja2 import Environment, meta
    env = Environment()
    ast = env.parse('{% extends "layout.html" %}{% include helper %}')
    referenced = list(meta.find_referenced_templates(ast))
    # referenced will be ['layout.html', None]
    ```

### Implementation Details

The `find_undeclared_variables` function internally uses a modified `CodeGenerator` to track variable lookups. The `find_referenced_templates` function traverses the AST for specific node types related to template inclusion and inheritance.
